channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: The 1930 Cord L-29 Cabriolet: A Milestone in Automotive Design and Technology
item.description: The 1930 Cord L-29 Cabriolet stands as a landmark in automotive history, notable for being the first American production car to feature front-wheel drive. Designed by Al Leamy under the direction of E...

item.title: Rohit Pawar condemns CJI’s remark likening youth to parasites
item.description: Karjat-Jamkhed MLA Rohit Pawar, a leader of the NCP (SP), strongly criticised a statement made by Chief Justice of India Surya Kant, in which he compared unemployed youth to parasites and cockroaches....

item.title: Leadway Assurance and FRSC Promote Road Safety, Reward Responsible Drivers
item.description: Leadway Assurance, one of Nigeria's top insurance companies, don join hand with the Federal Road Safety Corps (FRSC) to run the 'Arrive Alive' campaign wey dey encourage safe driving and proper road b...

item.title: Supreme Court Weakens Voting Rights Act, Undermining Minority Representation
item.description: In a landmark 6-3 decision in Louisiana v. Callais, the conservative majority of the U.S. Supreme Court severely weakened Section 2 of the Voting Rights Act, ruling that protecting minority representa...

item.title: India Moves Silver to Restricted Category to Control Surge in Imports
item.description: The Indian government has made it mandatory to obtain authorisation for importing silver, reclassifying the metal from the 'free' to the 'restricted' category. This decision follows a sharp surge in s...

item.title: Comelec reports over 4.7 million voter registration applications ahead of BSKE
item.description: The Commission on Elections (Comelec) reported that it has received a total of 4,740,551 voter registration applications from October 20, 2025, when the registration period began, up to May 9, 2026. T...

item.title: Dental Calculus Reveals Mercury Use in Medieval Leprosy Treatment
item.description: A recent study led by Dr. Elena Fiorin and published in the Journal of Archaeological Science investigated the use of mercury-based treatments for leprosy in the late medieval period. Unlike previous ...

item.title: Step-by-Step Guide to Making a Delicious Salmon Poke Bowl and Red Ice Cocktail at Home
item.description: This article na proper guide for anybody wey dey find healthy and tasty meal wey no go take plenty time. Salmon Poke Bowl, wey originally dey Hawaii, na like sushi wey dem scatter for bowl. E dey popu...

item.title: Orlando Pirates must beat Orbit College to secure Betway Premiership title
item.description: Orlando Pirates’ quest for their first Betway Premiership title in over a decade will be decided on the final day of the season against Orbit College at Mbombela Stadium. The Buccaneers dropped crucia...

item.title: Filipino Volunteers and Residents Celebrate Atin Ito Mission on Pag-asa Island
item.description: On May 3, 2026, the activist coalition Atin Ito successfully reached Pag-asa Island, also known internationally as Thitu Island, in the West Philippine Sea. The 37.2-hectare island, located about 480 ...

item.title: Venezuela Fury marries Noah Price in Isle of Man with unique bridal style and six-tiered cake
item.description: Venezuela Fury, aged 16, and her partner Noah Price, 19, celebrated their wedding at the Victorian Royal Chapel of St John's in the Isle of Man. The event, described by Molly-Mae Hague as 'beautiful',...

item.title: A Look Back at People and Their Simca Cars from the 1930s to 1960s
item.description: During the mid-20th century, Simca cars were more than just vehicles; they were symbols of freedom, prosperity, and personal style across Europe. From the compact Simca 5 to the sporty 1200S, these ca...

item.title: Historical Photographs of Thérèse of Lisieux Portraying Joan of Arc
item.description: Thérèse of Lisieux (1873–1897), also known as the Little Flower of Jesus, was a French Discalced Carmelite nun who is widely venerated today. Deeply inspired by the patriotic deeds of French heroines,...

item.title: Daily Entertainment Highlights from Sowetan – May 15, 2026
item.description: Sowetan’s daily lunchtime read for May 15, 2026, covers a variety of entertainment and sports stories. Rising star Nikita Mashishi talks about her latest acting role in the upcoming Mzansi Bioskop fil...

item.title: Nova Scotian Paul Boutilier, NHL and curling figure, remembered for humility and contributions to sport
item.description: Paul 'Boots' Boutilier, a native of Sydney, Nova Scotia, has passed away at the age of 63. Boutilier's life was marked by remarkable achievements both on and off the ice. As a defenceman, he was a fir...

item.title: B.C. couple hospitalised and isolated after presumptive hantavirus diagnosis
item.description: A couple in British Columbia is currently in hospital isolation after testing presumptive positive for the Andes hantavirus, linked to the cruise ship M.V. Hondius. The couple, among four Canadians co...

item.title: Visayas power grid placed on yellow alert due to high demand and plant shutdowns
item.description: MANILA, Philippines — The Visayas power grid was placed on yellow alert yesterday from 5 to 8 p.m. due to high electricity demand and the temporary shutdown of several major coal-fired power plants, t...

item.title: Semenyo’s decisive goal secures FA Cup victory for Manchester City over Chelsea
item.description: Manchester City claimed the FA Cup with a 1-0 victory over Chelsea, thanks to a stunning individual effort from Antoine Semenyo. The winning goal came in the 72nd minute when Semenyo, positioned in th...

item.title: Andhra Pradesh government announces financial incentives to encourage higher birth rates
item.description: Andhra Pradesh Chief Minister Chandrababu Naidu has announced that the state government will provide financial incentives to families for having more children, in an effort to address the declining bi...

item.title: Mitchell Johnson on the pressures facing AFL coaches and overreliance on data
item.description: Mitchell Johnson reflects on the intense pressures faced by AFL coaches, emphasising how the role often becomes a scapegoat for a club's underperformance. Using the recent departure of Michael Voss fr...
